title: tofieldia glutinosa subsp. japonica
intro: Tofieldia glutinosa subsp. japonica, also known as Japanese bog bean, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Tofieldiaceae. It is a perennial herb native to Japan and Korea, known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Asian medicine. The plant's roots and underground stems are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to their antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. Tofieldia glutinosa subsp. japonica can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are consumed to harness its medicinal benefits and promote overall well-being.
